27.reason: [This BFS implementation is recursive and incorrect. It should be iterative using a queue, but instead it recursively calls BFS on each neighbor, leading to wrong traversal order and potential infinite recursion. Also, it prints "0 " separately in main(), causing duplication or missing nodes.]

41.reason: [Recursive call to BFS inside BFS breaks the level-order traversal logic of BFS. This turns it into a DFS-like behavior and violates BFS semantics.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Recursive call to BFS inside BFS breaks the level-order traversal logic of BFS. This turns it into a DFS-like behavior and violates BFS semantics.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Recursive call to BFS inside BFS breaks standard BFS logic and corrupts traversal order because each recursive call processes its own neighbors but shares global queue and visited state inconsistently.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Recursive call to BFS inside BFS breaks standard BFS logic and corrupts traversal order because each recursive call processes its own neighbors but shares global queue and visited state inconsistently.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Recursive call to BFS inside BFS is wrong. This turns BFS into a hybrid DFS/BFS and breaks level-order traversal. Should process neighbors iteratively without recursion.]
